topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a stark intrusion into the quiet hum of my afternoon study session. It was a message from Mr. Harrison, my calculus instructor. My breath hitched in my throat, the sound loud in the otherwise silent library corner. My fingers froze above the keyboard, the screen reflecting a sudden sheen of sweat on my palms. I quickly minimized the app, my heart thumping a frantic rhythm against my ribs. I glanced up, half-expecting to see him lurking near the reference section, but the space was empty. A strange, cold prickle ran down my spine. I couldn't shake the feeling that I had somehow stumbled upon something private, something that shouldn't be visible.

[story 2]

I was walking home, the evening air cool against my skin, when my phone buzzed incessantly. It was a direct message from Ms. Evans, my English teacher. Every time the screen lit up, I felt a tightening in my chest, a sudden urge to duck behind a nearby tree. I kept glancing over my shoulder, half-expecting to see her emerging from the shadows. My steps quickened, a clumsy scramble down the sidewalk. *What is she doing?* The thought spun rapidly through my mind, making the familiar pavement seem suddenly treacherous. I shoved the phone into my pocket, focusing intensely on the distant streetlights, trying to regulate the rapid flutter beneath my sternum.

[story 3]

It happened during the mandatory online tutoring session. I was sharing my screen, reviewing a difficult historical document, when I noticed a familiar profile picture pop up in the corner of the shared window—Mr. Davies’ profile. My gaze snapped to the screen, and a wave of heat washed over my face. My hands started to tremble so badly I had to grip the edge of my desk. I swallowed hard, my mouth suddenly dry. I tried to look intently at the textbook, but my eyes kept darting back to the small, persistent icon. A deep sense of unease settled in my stomach, heavy and unwelcome, as I desperately tried to appear completely absorbed in the lesson, praying no one else noticed the slight rigidity in my posture.
